A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY FOUND A TORNADO WITH 75 TO 90 MPH WINDS IMPACTED THE EXTREME EASTERN SIDE OF MAPORT NAVAL STATION. SEVERAL NON-CRITICAL STRUCTURES WERE IMPACTED WITH MINOR DAMAGE. THE LENGTH OF THE TORNADO ACROSS THE BASE WAS .32 MILES HOWEVER IT IS LIKELY THE FUNNEL CONTINUED INTO THE RIVER AS A WATERSPOUT. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH...JUST BEFORE IT ENTERED THE RIVER...WAS 0.1 MILES. DAMAGE WAS GENERALLY CONFINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND BROKEN WINDOWS.
THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS DAMAGE FROM A TORNADO ON THE NAVAL BASE.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH IMPACTED AMEILA ISLAND LAST EVENING. THE STORM CROSSED THE BEACH ALONG A1A SOUTH OF RACHEAL AVE AND PROGRESSED NORTH-NORTHWEST NEAR AND EAST OF EGANS CREEK. THE TORNADO PASSED THROUGH THE STARBOARD LANDING NEIGHBORHOOD WITH 80 TO 100 MPH WINDS AND ENDED NEAR SUNNRISE DRIVE. THE PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 2.25 MILES AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 0.4 MILES OR ABOUT 2050 FEET NEAR JASMINE STREET.

THE OCALA STAR BANNER BANNER REPORTS THAT THE MARION COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE RECEIVED MULTIPLE CALLS OF DOWNED TREES AND POWERLINES IN THE VICINITY OF OCALA. MANY STREETS IN JESUP ARE FLOODED. A FEW STRUCTURES HAVE WATER FLOWING INTO THEM.
FLASH FLOODING IN JESUP. NUMEROUS ROADS FLOODED, INCLUDING MAIN ROAD 84. 4 VEHICLES COMPLETELY COVERED BY WATER. WATER ENTERING HOMES, MOTELS, FIRE STATIONS.

WAYNE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS 20 TO 30 HOMES IN DOWNTOWN JESUP FLOODED WITH APPROXIMATELY ONE FOOT OF STANDING WATER. APPROXIMATELY FOUR INCHES OF STANDING WATER WAS REPORTED AT THE MAIN FIRE STATION IN JESUP. ADDITIONAL STRUCTURES IN DOWNTOWN JESUP HAVE BEEN FLOODED...INCLUDING A MOTEL. U.S. HIGHWAY 84 WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING ON MONDAY EVENING...BUT HAS REOPENED TO TRAFFIC OVERNIGHT. WSAV-TV REPORTS THAT WAYNE COUNTY SCHOOLS WILL BE CLOSED ON TUESDAY DUE TO FLOODED ROADS.

EM REPORTED MULTIPLE TREES DOWN...POWERLINES DOWN...AND DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OF A BARN.
A TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN ALONG SUNFISH ROAD.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT THIS DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY A DOWNBURST WITH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ESTIMATED AT AROUND 100 MPH. 18 X 80 FOOT WOODEN BARN BUILDING DAMAGED AND MOVED OFF ITS CONCRETE FOUNDATION...WITH THE METAL ROOF OF THE BUILDING THROWN INTO A NEARBY TREE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. PENNY SIZED HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED.
18 X 80 FOOT WOODEN BARN BUILDING DAMAGED AND MOVED OFF ITS CONCRETE FOUNDATION...WITH THE METAL ROOF OF THE BUILDING THROWN INTO A NEARBY TREE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

AN EF1 TORNADO...WITH APPROXIMATE WINDS OF 95 TO 105 MPH WAS CONFIRMED BY A STORM ASSESSMENT TEAM. THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN NORTH OF ESPANOLA...THEN TRACKED NORTHEAST ACROSS THE B SECTION OF PALM COAT REACHING ITS MAXIMUM SIZE AND STRENGTH...WEAKENING SLIGHTLY AS THE TORNADO CROSSED THE F AND HAMMOCK SECTIONS OF PALM COAST.

FLAGLER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT RELAYED A REPORT OF A POSSIBLE TORNADO TOUCHDOWN ON BRITTANY LANE. REPORTS OF TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES IN THE AREA. ALSO REPORTS OF TOPS OF LARGE PINE TREES SNAPPED OFF AT THE INTERSECTION OF BANNER LANE AND BANNERWOOD LANE.
